title: HTTP Representations, Validators, Ranges, and File Transfer · Guided Systems Studio
summary: A delivery team incrementally builds HTTP file-representation and transfer contract, changes one file or failure, and keeps earlier HTTP, Go, accessibility, security, and resource-budget invariants active. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of HTTP file-representation and transfer contract while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define media type, content length, content encoding, language, disposition, filename parameters, and cache metadata as representation facts.
Apply ETag and Last-Modified validators with If-Match, If-None-Match, If-Range, 304, 412, and changed-state evidence.
Parse and evaluate single byte ranges, suffix ranges, satisfiable bounds, 206, Content-Range, Accept-Ranges, and 416.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Representation Metadata
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go file-delivery team handles case 9694 during a high-traffic evidence intake window. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate conditional inclusive byte ranges and response-body cleanup; keep browser execution deterministic and move AWS claims to an authorized transfer gate; then change region, range, or signed header and explain the exact failure boundary. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Representation Metadata.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for define media type, content length, content encoding, language, disposition, filename parameters, and cache metadata as representation facts. For S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Representation Metadata, keep Representation Metadata connected to executable Go storage and delivery decision evidence.
